commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer approval of the security-policy shape before merge; automation cannot supply the external TUN proxy proof.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete SSRF-boundary concern by using broad private-network allowance for a fake-IP proxy workaround.; Review finding: [P1] Keep the fake-IP opt-in narrower — extensions/volcengine/tts.ts:160 (sha=ce6b73ad3378ba6756c9631c6d1061eac433bac6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `messages.tts.providers.volcengine.allowPrivateNetwork` option and forwards it into both Volcengine Seed Speech and legacy TTS guarded HTTP calls.

PR surface: Source +18. Total +18 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was provided. Source inspection shows current Volcengine TTS uses guarded fetch with a hostname allowlist and current SSRF tests block `198.18.0.0/15` unless the fake-IP range flag is set, so the reported failure is plausible but not proven in a real TUN setup.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer approval of the security-policy shape before merge; automation cannot supply the external TUN proxy proof.

target.title: feat(volcengine): add allowPrivateNetwork option for TTS behind TUN proxy
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds `allowPrivateNetwork` boolean option (default `false`) to Volcengine TTS provider
- When enabled, passes `allowPrivateNetwork: true` to the SSRF guard in both Seed Speech and legacy TTS HTTP calls
- Configurable via `messages.tts.providers.volcengine.allowPrivateNetwork` in openclaw.json or programmatic config

## Motivation
When running behind a TUN proxy (e.g., Surge, Clash) that intercepts `198.18.x.x` addresses for fast DNS resolution, the built-in SSRF guard `fetchWithSsrFGuard` blocks these RFC 1918 addresses. This makes TTS fail silently. The flag lets users behind such proxies explicitly opt in to private-network connections.

## Changes
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/volcengine/tts.ts` | Add `allowPrivateNetwork` param to type + destructure in both TTS functions; conditional spread in `policy` |
| `extensions/volcengine/speech-provider.ts` | Read flag from raw config, pass through `readProviderConfig`, forward to `volcengineTTS()` call |

## Test plan
- [ ] Default (no flag): TTS works as before, SSRF guard blocks private IPs
- [ ] `allowPrivateNetwork: true`: TTS succeeds behind TUN proxy intercepting 198.18.x.x
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: geekhuashan
